behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—mocked embedding clients can return any shape and hide model/index mismatch. REAL-INFRA: yes—real embedding API configuration and real index schema are involved. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mocking often hardcodes expected dimensions, so failures from model/version drift are missed. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the API against the real embedding provider and a real vector index schema; submit representative queries and assert every produced embedding length exactly matches the live index dimension before upsert/search.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—unit-mocking search can miss ordering bugs at module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: not strictly required if using an in-repo/local vector DB fixture. MOCK-HIDE: yes—a mocked repository can always return sorted data. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a local test vector index with controlled vectors, execute real similarity search through the API search stack, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected cosine ranking.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—mock rerankers cannot validate real semantic reordering behavior. REAL-INFRA: usually yes (real LLM API). MOCK-HIDE: yes—deterministic mocks can always force a reorder and hide model failures. LLM-DEP: yes—correctness depends on real model judgment and can vary run-to-run. MULTI-STEP: no—single agentic rerank step.
behaviors.test_description: Using a fixed nuanced-query dataset, call the real LLM reranker over raw top-K retrieval results and assert that, across repeated runs, re-ranked order differs from baseline in a statistically meaningful fraction while preserving item set.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—synthetic vectors/mocks do not capture real embedding-space distance distributions. REAL-INFRA: yes—real embedding generation plus real vector search thresholding is needed. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mocked scores can trivially satisfy empty-result behavior. LLM-DEP: no (this is pre-rerank retrieval quality/threshold behavior). MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Run no-match queries against a real embedding model and real vector DB with production-like thresholds; assert API returns an empty list when top similarity is below cutoff and does not return low-score filler results.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—mocked dependencies eliminate real network/model latency and contention. REAL-INFRA: yes—requires real HTTP path, real embedding call, real vector search, and real rerank invocation timing. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mock timings are not representative. LLM-DEP: output semantics are not under test; latency is infra/perf behavior. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like load test against the deployed API path using real downstream services, collect end-to-end request durations for a representative query mix, and assert median (p50) latency is < 2.0s over the run window.
